---
title: "Port lists"
description: "Reference for the port presets used by scanning tools"
---

Port presets define which ports get scanned in each mode. Instead of testing all 65,535 possible ports, each preset covers a selection of ports ranked by how often they appear open on internet-facing systems. Larger presets mean better coverage but longer scan times.

## Tools using port presets

| Tool | Presets | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| [Port Scanner](/tools/port-scanner) | Light, Deep, custom Top 10/100/1000/5000 | TCP and UDP |
| [Network Scanner](/tools/network-scanner) | Light, Deep, custom Top 10/100/1000/5000, OpenVAS default | TCP and UDP |
| [SSL/TLS Scanner](/tools/ssl-tls-scanner) | Custom Top 10/100/1000/5000 | TCP only |
| [Sniper](/tools/sniper) | Custom Top 100/1000/5000 | TCP only |
| [Kubernetes Scanner](/tools/kubernetes-scanner) | Light (K8s-specific), Deep, custom Top 10/100/1000/5000 | TCP only; Light Top 10 uses a K8s-specific list |
| [Password Auditor](/tools/password-auditor) | Top 100 TCP (fixed default) | Custom port list also accepted |

<Note>
TCP and UDP use separate port lists. TCP lists prioritize web (80, 443), SSH (22), and database ports. UDP lists prioritize DNS (53), DHCP (67-68), SNMP (161-162), and NTP (123).
</Note>

## TCP port lists

### Light and Deep fixed presets

Port Scanner and Network Scanner Light and Deep scans use fixed TCP port lists.

### Custom presets

These presets are available in Custom scan mode for Port Scanner, Network Scanner, SSL/TLS Scanner, and Sniper. Password Auditor uses the Top 100 list by default.

<Note>
Sniper supports Top 100, Top 1000, and Top 5000. It does not support the Top 10 preset.
</Note>
